mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production signal expanding real output, which lifts the neutral real rate and pushes long-term Treasury yields higher over the following month irrespective of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO level and 20-day ahead yield change is below +0.10, or the mean yield change following INDPRO increases is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0%, it signals slack that reduces wage pressures and inflation expectations, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day yield change following UNRATE crossing above 5.0% is positive on average over any two-year window, or the hit rate is below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with strong payroll gains pushes the Fed toward tightening; this repricing of the expected path of the policy rate lifts the 10-year yield over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month > 0.3% and PAYEMS > 250K in the same release month
failure_condition: Average 20-day yield change in triggered months is ≤ 0, or cumulative three-year Rank IC is ≤ 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts, as a cyclical demand proxy, signal future growth; in a rising-rate regime (FEDFUNDS above 5.0%) further increases in housing starts would indicate overheating and push yields up, but in an easing regime (FEDFUNDS falling) the same data would amplify a bear-flattening impulse and depress yields.
failure_condition: If the slope of yield change vs HOUST surprise is positive in high-rate regimes over any 3-year sample, or the conditional IC is not significant at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Large positive surprises in retail sales signal robust consumer demand, which increases real rate expectations and raises the 10-year yield within five trading days as markets reprice the growth outlook.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day yield change following large retail sales surprises is not positive (t-test p > 0.10) over a multi-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated federal funds rate, when combined with falling inflation, signals that the Fed will eventually ease; the front-end eases but the long end falls as term premium declines, lowering the 10-year yield over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: late monetary tightening cycle with disinflation
failure_condition: 60-day yield change in triggered quarters is not reliably negative (Rank IC < 0 in two-thirds of rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: Very low unemployment (UNRATE below 4.0%) in an environment of rising payrolls forces labor costs higher, elevating inflation compensation and pushing the 10-year yield up over a short 5-day window.
failure_condition: Average 5-day yield change after such triggers is non-positive, or false positive rate above 60% as measured by positive yields in non-tight labor conditions.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production contracts for two consecutive months, the supply-side weakening signals a slowdown that reduces inflation expectations and real growth, leading to a lower 10-year yield over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month negative for 2 consecutive releases
failure_condition: Negative yield change in triggered periods is not observed in >50% of cases, or the average change is positive.
